name: Valle de Ansó - Refugio de Zuriza desde Berdún
name_original: Valle de Ansó – Refugio de Zuriza desde Berdún
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Canal De Berdún traverse a diverse landscape within the Aragonese Pyrenees, characterized by river valleys and winding mountain passes. The region offers varied terrain, from moderate rides to challenging excursions with significant elevation gains. Cyclists encounter a mix of natural beauty and historical villages, with roads often leading through areas of lower altitude Pyrenean environment. The area serves as a gateway to the broader Pyrenees, known for its challenging climbs. Best road cycling routes around Canal De Berdún The most popular road cycling route is Valle de Ansó - Refugio de Zuriza desde Berdún, a 48.3 miles (77.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbing through the Pyrenean foothills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Collado de Argibiela – Pierre Saint-Martin Pass loop from Parking Foz de Fago, a difficult 70.2 miles (113.0 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, leading through mountain passes with panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Jasa – Refugio de la Loma de Aísa loop from Canal de Berdún, a 74.7 miles (120.3 km) trail leading through varied Pyrenean terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling around Canal De Berdún is defined by river valleys, peaceful mountain passes, and the distinctive Pyrenean environment. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 2 easy, 13 moderate, and 24 difficult routes. The routes in Canal De Berdún are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Canal De Berdún's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Canal De Berdún?
A: There are nearly 40 road cycling routes around Canal De Berdún, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 2 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 24 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Canal De Berdún?
A: Road cycling routes in Canal De Berdún traverse diverse landscapes within the Aragonese Pyrenees. You can expect a mix of river valleys, winding mountain passes with hairpin bends, and areas of natural beauty. While many roads offer a serene climbing experience with minimal traffic, some asphalt on the Aragonese side of certain passes might be in poor condition, adding to the authentic Pyrenean challenge.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Canal De Berdún?
A: Yes, Canal De Berdún offers a few routes suitable for beginners. There are 2 easy road cycling routes available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful scenery without overly demanding climbs.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 24 difficult routes. Notable challenging options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927630101/valle-de-anso-refugio-de-zuriza-desde-berdun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Ansó - Refugio de Zuriza desde Berdún&lt;/a&gt;, a 48.3-mile (77.7 km) trail with substantial climbing, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33752170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Argibiela – Pierre Saint-Martin Pass loop from Parking Foz de Fago&lt;/a&gt;, a 70.2-mile (113.0 km) path with significant elevation gain through mountain passes.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while road cycling in Canal De Berdún?
A: The region is rich in historical and natural attractions. You might cycle past the medieval village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4612964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ansó&lt;/a&gt;, known for its traditional Pyrenean architecture, or the historic crossroads of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Reina de Jaca&lt;/a&gt;. The village of Berdún itself, perched atop a hill, offers impressive views and features like the 16th-century Gothic Santa Eulalia Church. Many routes also provide panoramic views of the Aragón River Valley and the surrounding Pyrenean landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Canal De Berdún?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to peaceful mountain passes, and the opportunity to explore charming historical villages nestled within striking natural landscapes. The challenging climbs and impressive panoramic views are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Canal De Berdún?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Canal De Berdún are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jasa – Refugio de la Loma de Aísa loop from Canal de Berdún&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40299388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Matamachos – Burgui loop from Canal de Berdún&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied Pyrenean terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Canal De Berdún?
A: The region's Pyrenean environment suggests that spring, summer, and early autumn are generally the most favorable seasons for road cycling. During these months, you can expect more pleasant temperatures and less chance of snow or ice on higher passes, allowing for better exploration of the winding roads and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there options for accommodation or places to eat along the routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs on every route are not detailed, the region features charming villages where time seems to stand still. Berdún itself, as the main village, and nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4612964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ansó&lt;/a&gt; are likely to offer options for refreshments and accommodation, catering to visitors exploring the area.

Q: Can I reach Canal De Berdún using public transport with my bike?
A: Information on specific public transport options for bikes to Canal De Berdún is not readily available. However, as a gateway to the Pyrenees, it's advisable to research local bus or train services in the Aragon region and their policies regarding bicycle transport, especially if traveling from larger cities like Huesca or Zaragoza.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in Canal De Berdún or the surrounding Pyrenees?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads in the Canal De Berdún area or the broader Pyrenees. However, always respect local regulations, especially if routes pass through protected natural areas or private land. It's always good practice to check for any local advisories before your ride.
tips: Nice section that runs through the Foz de Biniés ravine. The road passes through several stone tunnels and viewpoints, sandwiched between vertical rock walls.

A beautiful medieval village with traditional Pyrenean architecture. Visit the 16th-century medieval tower, the Virgen de Puyeta hermitage, the San Pedro church, and the museums. There are also several restaurants where you can enjoy typical local cuisine.

Next to the Zuriza refuge (without services) is the Zuriza campsite, which is one of the most beautiful I have ever visited. Surrounded by large peaks, far from any urban center and close to the Veral river for bathing, it is a true paradise.
Spending the night here is a very good option and if you are just passing through, perhaps the bar will come in handy for a drink before continuing with the route.

Just when the road crosses the river Veral, at the height of the Zuriza refuge, there is a bathing area with a certain depth in which to cool off. The river esplanade continues parallel to the road as we climb towards the Zuriza campsite, so there are plenty of areas to get your feet wet and beat the heat on summer days.

waypoints.name: Swimming area in the Veral River
